title: UDLM Resource Type Specification
description: Meta-schema that every Resource Type Specification in the UDLM Resource Type Registry MUST validate against. JSON Schema 2020-12 is the normative wire format for entity-type definitions (contracts/schema-sharing.md). A Resource Type Specification is the versioned, formal definition of a Resource Type: field schema, constraints, lifecycle rules, portability classification, typed realized outputs, and allowed relationship types (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). This meta-schema is itself part of the UDLM spec and is versioned on the SPEC axis.

$id.description: Canonical, project-controlled identifier on udlm.dev (HTTPS-enforced via the .dev HSTS preload). Encodes BOTH version axes and mirrors `conforms_to`: https://udlm.dev/registry/udlm/<spec>/<TypeName>/<version>. TypeName is `Category.Type` for the Resource family (Compute.VirtualMachine) or single-segment for the Knowledge family (Capability). Relationships reference a target by its `$id`. A new MAJOR mints a new `$id`.

conforms_to.description: SPEC-axis binding: the UDLM spec major.minor this type is written against (its apiVersion). Peers conformant to the same MAJOR are wire-compatible (CONFORMANCE.md §9).

uuid.description: RFC 9562 UUIDv4 identity of this type specification (contracts/identifier-scheme.md §2.1 — the normative UUID standard: v4 identity / v7 time-ordered / others prohibited). Immutable for the life of the type.

resource_type.description: Type name, unique within its `family`. Domain-owned types: namespaced Category.Type (Compute.VirtualMachine, Network.IPAddress). Cross-cutting / foundational types not owned by a single domain: single-segment (Capability, TaxonomyTerm, Topology) — disambiguated by `family` (registry/naming-conventions.md §1, entities/knowledge-family.md).

version.description: ENTITY-axis version: this type's own Major.Minor.Revision (see registry/VERSIONING.md). Immutable once published; any change produces a new version.

family.description: Entity-type family (foundations/entity-type-families.md) — a logical grouping for discovery, NOT a usage boundary. Resource = maintained states; Process = bounded executions; Knowledge = information; Access = identity.

entity_type.description: The coarse sub-type within the family. Resource & Process: Atomic (owns no constituents) | Composite (owns constituents — a Composite declares constituents[] at the realized layer). Knowledge: Capability | TaxonomyTerm | Alias | Antipattern | UseCase. Access: Identity. Constrained by `family` (see allOf). The specific type (e.g. Compute.VirtualMachine, Automation.Workflow) is `resource_type`, not `entity_type`. (foundations/entity-types.md, entities/knowledge-family.md.)

lifecycle_archetype.description: How this type interprets the universal four states: `provisioning` (Resource — Intent→Requested→Realized→Discovered via a provider) or `curation` (Knowledge — proposed→under-review→canonical→deprecated). Optional; inferred from `family` if absent.

portability.description: Portability classification (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). Any deviation from full portability MUST be explicitly declared.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this version (foundations/layering-and-versioning.md universal deprecation model).

date.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source.

deprecated_in_version.description: ENTITY version at which deprecation was declared (Redfish 'versionDeprecated' analog) — lets consumers know how long an element has been deprecated relative to the version they consume.

deprecation.description: REQUIRED when status is deprecated or retired. A breaking (MAJOR) successor MUST supply migration_guidance.

origination_timestamp.description: When this type was originated — RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps). The producing clock is attributed via the nearest time_source. Backfilled 2026-07-05 from each file's first git commit instant (the auditable carrier).

spec.description: The field schema for the Intent/Requested state — the wire contract every provider offering this type MUST satisfy. A nested JSON Schema 2020-12 fragment. Catalog items / constraint profiles (E1) project over these fields by path; they never widen them. This base is CLOSED (a type's `spec` sets `additionalProperties: false`): a provider EXTENDS an instance additively via the realized entity's provider-namespaced `provider_extensions` surface, never by widening this base — no-override is structural (ADR-PROV-004, closes #198).

immutable.description: Dot-paths into `spec` that are createOnly — set at Intent, never changed post-realization; a change forces replace → supersession. Use for sovereignty-determining fields (zone / classification). Declarative noun; DCM enforces it (R3 / cross-cutting P4).

outputs.description: Typed Realized-state outputs (E2): named values this type publishes when it reaches Realized. Other entities bind to these via dependency references; the binding is contract-checked against these types rather than string-spliced. Output NAMES are snake_case (propertyNames pattern) — they are the referenceable, typed binding surface: a catalog-item binding names a producer output by this key, and the validator resolves it against this list (data-model-core §2 [D8.3] typed outputs).

relationships.description: Relationship TEMPLATES this resource illustrates — guidance and placement hints, NOT a closed allowlist (UDLM ADR-009 §4). A catalog item or provider MAY declare relationships not listed here; the platform cannot pre-validate what a provider will create. Only relationships marked enforcement=structural are required/validated (REL-002); all others are examples (REL-001 advisory). entities/entity-relationships.md, service-dependencies.md.

enforcement.description: example (default) = an illustrative/placement-hint relationship a provider may satisfy, override, or ignore (REL-001 advisory). structural = an invariant true by the nature of the resource (a dataset cannot exist without its pool) — required and validated (REL-002). UDLM ADR-009 §4.

target_field.description: An `outputs` key on the target this edge binds to (e.g. a database's `connection_string`). The binding is a typed reference resolved at dispatch — data movement, not transformation (T4).

name.description: Machine-readable relation identity — the type's declared relation vocabulary (common-elements §9). Instance edges cite it via dependencies[].relation and MUST use a declared name (REL-001). A relation refines its kind's semantics and never overrides them (REL-003) — graph consumers that ignore relation still act correctly on kind. REQUIRED when a type declares more than one relationship of the same kind whose instance edges would otherwise be ambiguous.

adopted_from.description: The external standard defining this relation name (e.g. 'RFC 8343 lower-layer-if', 'OASIS TOSCA ConnectsTo') — SHOULD correspond to an entry in the type's adopts[]. Relation names are adopted, not invented, wherever a standard names the concept (common-elements §9).

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle rules. Defaults to UDLM's four-state model Intent → Requested → Realized → Discovered (foundations/four-states.md). Override only to declare type-specific decommission behavior.

adopts.description: External standards this type ADOPTS by reference (core-tenets T5, design-principles/adopted-standards.md). The standard's schema is NOT restated here — only an identity join + a version-pinned conformance pointer. Negotiating/translating versions is Policy (DCM), never the data model.

aliases.description: Alternative names this type is known by (AKA / cross-walk for compatibility), for names NOT already covered by an `adopts[].standard_name`. Use for vendor/colloquial names that do not correspond to a formally-adopted standard (e.g. AWS 'Dedicated Host', OpenStack 'Ironic node'). Names from standards this type formally adopts belong on `adopts[].standard_name` instead.

standard.description: OPTIONAL: the standard/vendor/ecosystem this name comes from, e.g. AWS, OpenStack.

metering.description: PROPOSED (cost-metering-linkage.md; ADR-COST-002). The meterable cost SURFACE — WHAT about this resource is measurable and WHERE each value comes from — the inputs a cost engine consumes. UDLM carries the surface only; rates and the calculation are the engine's (ADR-COST-001). This is one side of the reciprocal contract: UDLM/DCM hands these dimensions out; the engine hands cost back.

name.description: snake_case dimension name, e.g. allocated_vcpu, power_kwh.

unit.description: Unit of measure, e.g. vcpu, GB, hours, kWh, cpu_hours.

source.description: Where the value is resolved from. Deliberately no 'formula'/'derived' kind — UDLM declares sources, never computes (ADR-COST-001). A realization resolves each source; the engine does the math.

kind.description: field = read a spec field / outputs key; lifecycle = derived from four-state timestamps; telemetry = looked up from a serve_data telemetry Information Provider.

ref.description: For kind=field: dot-path to a spec field or `outputs` key. For kind=lifecycle: a named interval, e.g. realized_to_decommissioned.

metric.description: For kind=telemetry: the metric name the realization looks up from a telemetry provider, e.g. vm.cpu.usage.

priced_by.description: PROPOSED (cost-metering-linkage.md; ADR-COST-002). Cost-model REFERENCE: associates this type with an external cost model in a serve_data:cost provider. UDLM never defines the model's contents (ADR-COST-001). On a type = the default; a realized entity may override it (per-customer/contract pricing, whose lifecycle stays external). Which reference wins is admin POLICY, not data.

information_provider_uuid.description: The registered serve_data:cost provider (the cost engine).

external_model_id.description: Opaque id of the cost model within that engine. UDLM treats it as a black box.

standard_version.description: A reference over an external standard's OWN versioning scheme — a pin ("1.4"), a wildcard ("1.x"), or a range expression (">=1.2 <2.0"). The schema only checks it is a non-empty string; RANGE RESOLUTION / negotiation is Policy (DCM), not the data model (T2/T5).

adopted_standard_ref.description: A reference to an external standard this entity adopts (T5; design-principles/adopted-standards.md §3.1). Carries provenance + version pointer + recorded license verdict — never the standard's schema. `identity_join` is present only for data-join adoptions (e.g. FOCUS cost records keyed by ResourceId); reference-vocabulary adoptions (Redfish/Metal3/DNS field names) omit it. Provenance (`source`) and the license verdict (`license` + `license_compatibility`) are MANDATORY (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §22–23).

standard.description: External standard name, e.g. FOCUS, OpenCost, Redfish, Metal3, OSCAL, SCIM, OpenTelemetry.

standard_name.description: OPTIONAL cross-walk: the name THIS standard uses for this type/concept (e.g. Redfish 'ComputerSystem', Metal3 'BareMetalHost'). Together with `standard` + `version` this is the {alternative name, spec, spec version} translation for compatibility — a consumer using the standard's vocabulary can resolve its term to this UDLM type.

role.description: What the adopted data/vocabulary is for (e.g. 'cost-and-usage data for this resource').

identity_join.description: OPTIONAL. The anchor only UDLM can provide for a DATA-join adoption: a local identity field bound to a column in the standard's records (e.g. uuid -> FOCUS ResourceId). Omit for reference-vocabulary adoptions that borrow field names without joining to the standard's records.

local_field.description: Field on this entity used as the join key (e.g. uuid).

standard_column.description: Column/attribute in the standard's records that the local field joins to (e.g. ResourceId).

source.description: Canonical URL of the standard (e.g. https://focus.finops.org/). REQUIRED provenance (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §22).

license.description: The source's license identifier (SPDX where possible, e.g. CC-BY-4.0, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0). REQUIRED (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §23).

license_compatibility.description: Recorded verdict of the license check against UDLM's Apache-2.0 (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §23): `compatible-reference` = we only reference the vocabulary (field names = facts), license-clean regardless of source license (the default); `compatible-vendor` = source is Apache-2.0-compatible, so its text MAY be absorbed/vendored; `reference-only` = copyleft/file-scoped source (GPL/LGPL/MPL) whose names MAY be referenced but whose text/files MUST NOT be vendored.

disposition.description: Adoption tier (adopted-standards.md §1a). `tier1_value` = a value/codelist/vocabulary reference — near-constant, does NOT version in a way that changes shape (a referenced field constraint). `tier2_record` = a record/schema whose SHAPE this type conforms to — it versions its shape, needs the ADS apparatus (identity join + provider support matrix + version pin), and the type CANNOT change its conforming shape without breaking conformance.
